steps.title: Translate "at least 20 items"
steps.content:
With $x$ workbooks and $y$ flash card sets, the total number of items is $x + y$. "At least 20" means the total is greater than or equal to 20:
$$x + y \geq 20$$

steps.title: Translate "total cost not over 80 dollars"
steps.content:
Workbooks cost 3 dollars each and flash cards cost 4 dollars per set, so the total cost in dollars is $3x + 4y$. "Not over 80 dollars" means less than or equal to 80:
$$3x + 4y \leq 80$$

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
The system is $x + y \geq 20$ together with $3x + 4y \leq 80$.
  • $x + y \ge 20$ and $3x + 4y \le 80$: $x+y \ge 20$ and $3x+4y \le 80$ — matches both inequalities exactly. Correct.
  • $x + y \ge 20$ and $3x + 4y \ge 80$: $x+y \ge 20$ and $3x+4y \ge 80$ — reverses the cost inequality, allowing orders that exceed 80 dollars. Eliminated.
  • $x + 4y \le 20$ and $x + y \ge 80$: $x+4y \le 20$ and $x+y \ge 80$ — mixes up the coefficients and reverses both directions. Eliminated.
  • $x + y \le 20$ and $3x + 4y \ge 80$: $x+y \le 20$ and $3x+4y \ge 80$ — reverses both inequalities, so it caps the count and requires a minimum cost. Eliminated.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
